name: sphinx-autoapi
documentation:
# Sphinx AutoAPI

```{image} https://readthedocs.org/projects/sphinx-autoapi/badge/?version=latest
:alt: Documentation
:target: https://sphinx-autoapi.readthedocs.org
```

```{image} https://github.com/readthedocs/sphinx-autoapi/actions/workflows/main.yml/badge.svg?branch=main
:alt: Github Build Status
:target: https://github.com/readthedocs/sphinx-autoapi/actions/workflows/main.yml?query=branch%3Amain
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/sphinx-autoapi.svg
:alt: PyPI Version
:target: https://pypi.org/project/sphinx-autoapi/
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/sphinx-autoapi.svg
:alt: Supported Python Versions
:target: https://pypi.org/project/sphinx-autoapi/
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/badge/code%20style-black-000000.svg
:alt: Formatted with Black
:target: https://github.com/python/black
```

Sphinx AutoAPI is a Sphinx extension for generating complete API documentation
without needing to load, run, or import the project being documented.

In contrast to the traditional [Sphinx autodoc](https://www.sphinx-doc.org/en/master/usage/extensions/autodoc.html),
which requires manual authoring and uses code imports,
AutoAPI finds and generates documentation by parsing source code.

For more information, see [the full documentation](https://sphinx-autoapi.readthedocs.org).

## Getting Started

The following steps will walk through how to add AutoAPI to an existing Sphinx project.
For instructions on how to set up a Sphinx project,
see [Sphinx's documentation](https://www.sphinx-doc.org/en/master/usage/quickstart.html).

### Installation

AutoAPI can be installed through pip:

```bash
pip install sphinx-autoapi
```

Next, add and configure AutoAPI in your Sphinx project's `conf.py`.

```python
extensions.append('autoapi.extension')

autoapi_dirs = ['path/to/source/files', 'src']
```

When the documentation is built,
AutoAPI will now generate API documentation into an `autoapi/` directory
and add an entry to the documentation in your top level table of contents!

To configure AutoAPI behaviour further,
see the [Configuration documentation](https://sphinx-autoapi.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/config.html).

## Contributing

### Running the tests

Tests are executed through [tox](https://tox.readthedocs.io/en/latest/).

```bash
tox
```

### Code Style

Code is formatted using [black](https://github.com/python/black).

You can check your formatting using black's check mode:

```bash
tox -e format
```

You can also get black to format your changes for you:

```bash
black autoapi/ tests/
```

You can even get black to format changes automatically when you commit using [pre-commit](https://pre-commit.com/):

```bash
pip install pre-commit
pre-commit install
```

### Release Notes

Release notes are managed through [towncrier](https://towncrier.readthedocs.io/en/stable/index.html).
When making a pull request you will need to create a news fragment to document your change:

```bash
tox -e release_notes -- create --help
```

## Versioning

We use [SemVer](https://semver.org/) for versioning. For the versions available, see the [tags on this repository](https://github.com/readthedocs/sphinx-autoapi/tags).

## License

This project is licensed under the MIT License.
See the [LICENSE.rst](LICENSE.rst) file for details.

modules.name: autoapi.extension
documentation:
Sphinx Auto-API Top-level Extension.

This extension allows you to automagically generate API documentation from your project.

functions.name: autoapi.extension.run_autoapi
documentation: Load AutoAPI data from the filesystem.

functions.name: autoapi.extension.doctree_read
documentation: Inject AutoAPI into the TOC Tree dynamically.

variables.name: autoapi.extension._VIEWCODE_CACHE
documentation: Caches a module's parse results for use in viewcode.

modules.name: autoapi
documentation: Sphinx AutoAPI

modules.name: autoapi.settings
documentation:
Basic settings for AutoAPI projects.

You shouldn't need to touch this.

modules.name: autoapi.directives
documentation: AutoAPI directives

classes.name: autoapi.directives.AutoapiSummary
documentation: A version of autosummary that uses static analysis.

classes.name: autoapi.directives.NestedParse
documentation:
Nested parsing to remove the first heading of included rST

This is used to handle the case where we like to remove user supplied
headings from module docstrings. This is required to reduce the number of
duplicate headings on sections.

classes.name: autoapi._objects.PythonObject

methods.name: autoapi._objects.PythonObject.__getstate__
documentation: Obtains serialisable data for pickling.

methods.name: autoapi._objects.PythonObject.rendered
documentation: Shortcut to render an object in templates.

methods.name: autoapi._objects.PythonObject.__lt__
documentation: Object sorting comparison

methods.name: autoapi._objects.PythonObject.short_name
documentation: Shorten name property

methods.name: autoapi._objects.PythonObject.output_dir
documentation: The directory to render this object.

methods.name: autoapi._objects.PythonObject.output_filename
documentation: The name of the file to render into, without a file suffix.

methods.name: autoapi._objects.PythonObject.include_path
documentation:
Return 'absolute' path without regarding OS path separator

This is used in `toctree` directives, as Sphinx always expects Unix
path separators

methods.name: autoapi._objects.PythonObject.docstring
documentation:
The docstring for this object.

If a docstring did not exist on the object,
this will be the empty string.

For classes, this will also depend on the
{confval}`autoapi_python_class_content` option.

methods.name: autoapi._objects.PythonObject.is_top_level_object
documentation:
Whether this object is at the very top level (True) or not (False).

This will be False for subpackages and submodules.

methods.name: autoapi._objects.PythonObject.is_undoc_member
documentation: Whether this object has a docstring (False) or not (True).

methods.name: autoapi._objects.PythonObject.is_private_member
documentation: Whether this object is private (True) or not (False).

methods.name: autoapi._objects.PythonObject.is_special_member
documentation: Whether this object is a special member (True) or not (False).

methods.name: autoapi._objects.PythonObject.display
documentation:
Whether this object should be displayed in documentation.

This attribute depends on the configuration options given in
{confval}`autoapi_options` and the result of {event}`autoapi-skip-member`.

methods.name: autoapi._objects.PythonObject.summary
documentation:
The summary line of the docstring.

The summary line is the first non-empty line, as-per {pep}`257`.
This will be the empty string if the object does not have a docstring.

class_variables.name: autoapi._objects.PythonObject.member_order
documentation: The ordering of objects when doing "groupwise" sorting.

A class representing an entity from the parsed source code.

This class turns the dictionaries output by the parser into an object.

Args:
    obj: JSON object representing this object
    jinja_env: A template environment for rendering this object

The representation of a class.

classes.name: autoapi._objects.PythonException

functions.name: autoapi._astroid_utils.resolve_import_alias
documentation:
Resolve a name from an aliased import to its original name.

Args:
    name: The potentially aliased name to resolve.
    import_names: The pairs of original names and aliases from the import.

Returns:
    The original name.

functions.name: autoapi._astroid_utils.get_full_import_name
documentation:
Get the full path of a name from a `from x import y` statement.

Args:

: import_from: The astroid node to resolve the name of.
  name: The short name or alias of what was imported.

  > This is `y` in `from x import y`
  > and `z` in `from x import y as z`.

Returns:

: str: The full import path of the name.

functions.name: autoapi._astroid_utils.resolve_qualname
documentation:
Resolve where a node is defined to get its fully qualified name.

Args:
    node: The node representing the base name.
    basename: The partial base name to resolve.

Returns:
    The fully resolved base name.

functions.name: autoapi._astroid_utils.get_full_basenames
documentation:
Resolve the partial names of a class' bases to fully qualified names.

Args:
    node: The class definition node to resolve the bases of.

Returns:
    The fully qualified names.

functions.name: autoapi._astroid_utils.get_assign_value
documentation:
Get the name and value of the assignment of the given node.

Assignments to multiple names are ignored, as per PEP 257.

Args:
    node: The node to get the assignment value from.

Returns:
    The name that is assigned to, and the string representation of
    the value assigned to the name (if it can be converted).

functions.name: autoapi._astroid_utils.get_assign_annotation
documentation:
Get the type annotation of the assignment of the given node.

Args:
    node: The node to get the annotation for.

Returns:
    The type annotation as a string, or None if one does not exist.

functions.name: autoapi._astroid_utils.is_decorated_with_property
documentation:
Check if the function is decorated as a property.

Args:
    node: The node to check.

Returns:
    True if the function is a property, False otherwise.

functions.name: autoapi._astroid_utils.is_decorated_with_property_setter
documentation:
Check if the function is decorated as a property setter.

Args:
    node: The node to check.

Returns:
    True if the function is a property setter, False otherwise.

functions.name: autoapi._astroid_utils.is_decorated_with_overload
documentation:
Check if the function is decorated as an overload definition.

Args:
    node: The node to check.

Returns:
    True if the function is an overload definition, False otherwise.

functions.name: autoapi._astroid_utils.is_constructor
documentation:
Check if the function is a constructor.

Args:
    node: The node to check.

Returns:
    True if the function is a constructor, False otherwise.

functions.name: autoapi._astroid_utils.is_exception
documentation:
Check if a class is an exception.

Args:
    node: The node to check.

Returns:
    True if the class is an exception, False otherwise.

functions.name: autoapi._astroid_utils.is_local_import_from
documentation:
Check if a node is an import from the local package.

Args:
    node: The node to check.
    package_name: The name of the local package.

Returns:
    True if the node is an import from the local package. False otherwise.

functions.name: autoapi._astroid_utils.get_module_all
documentation:
Get the contents of the `__all__` variable from a module.

Args:

: node: The module to get `__all__` from.

Returns:

: The contents of `__all__` if defined. Otherwise `None`.

functions.name: autoapi._astroid_utils.get_return_annotation
documentation:
Get the return annotation of a node.

Args:
    node: The node to get the return annotation for.

Returns:
    The return annotation of the given node.

functions.name: autoapi._astroid_utils.get_class_docstring
documentation:
Get the docstring of a node, using a parent docstring if needed.

Args:
    node: The node to get a docstring for.

Returns:
    The docstring of the class, or the empty string if no docstring
    was found or defined.

functions.name: autoapi._mapper._expand_wildcard_placeholder
documentation:
Expand a wildcard placeholder to a sequence of named placeholders.

```
param original_module

The data dictionary of the module
that the placeholder is imported from.

type original_module

dict

param originals_map

A map of the names of children under the module
to their data dictionaries.

type originals_map

dict(str, dict)

param placeholder

The wildcard placeholder to expand.

type placeholder

dict

returns

The placeholders that the wildcard placeholder represents.

rtype

list(dict)
```

functions.name: autoapi._mapper._resolve_module_placeholders
documentation:
Resolve all placeholder children under a module.

Args:
    modules (dict(str, dict)): A mapping of module names to their
        data dictionary. Placeholders are resolved in place.
    module_name (str): The name of the module to resolve.
    visit_path: An ordered set of visited module names.
    visited (collections.OrderedDict)
    resolved (set(str)): A set of already resolved module names.

functions.name: autoapi._mapper._resolve_placeholder
documentation:
Resolve a placeholder to the given original object.

Args:
    placeholder (dict): The placeholder to resolve, in place.
    original (dict): The object that the placeholder represents.

classes.name: autoapi._mapper.Mapper

methods.name: autoapi._mapper.Mapper.load
documentation:
Load objects from the filesystem into the `paths` dictionary

Also include an attribute on the object, `relative_path` which is the
shortened, relative path the package/module

methods.name: autoapi._mapper.Mapper.read_file
documentation:
Read file input into memory, returning deserialized objects

Args:
    path: Path of file to read

methods.name: autoapi._mapper.Mapper._resolve_placeholders
documentation: Resolve objects that have been imported from elsewhere.

methods.name: autoapi._mapper.Mapper._hide_yo_kids
documentation: For all direct children of a module/package, hide them if needed.

methods.name: autoapi._mapper.Mapper._create_module_hierarchy
documentation: Populate the sub{module,package}s attributes of all top level objects.

methods.name: autoapi._mapper.Mapper._render_selection
documentation: Propagate display values to children.

methods.name: autoapi._mapper.Mapper.create_class
documentation:
Create a class from the passed in data

Args:
    data: dictionary data of parser output

Base class for mapping `PythonMapperBase` objects to Sphinx.

Args:
    app: Sphinx application instance
